text.title: gitops-progressive-delivery Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for gitops-progressive-delivery as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using progressive delivery for schema migrations that change database columns used by both versions simultaneously.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Two versions of the app running concurrently means both the old schema and the new schema must be valid simultaneously. Skipping this constraint causes data corruption or runtime errors during the canary window.

failure-mode.description: Setting maxWeight: 100 with a single large step (e.g., stepWeight: 100) — effectively a standard rolling update with analysis theatre.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Shifts 100% of traffic immediately, removing the blast-radius benefit of progressive delivery. Use at least 5-10 steps to ensure rollback has time to fire before all users are on the bad version.
